# Purpose
This code defines a function [`action_chaining_window_push`](<#action_chaining_window_push>) that is part of a C source file. The function is intended to push a window onto a `WindowStack` with specific parameters, including a `title`, an `action_group`, and callback functions for menu selection and closure events. The function takes pointers to callback functions `select_cb` and `closed_cb`, along with their respective contexts, `select_cb_context` and `closed_cb_context`. However, the function currently does not perform any operations and immediately returns. The file includes a header `action_chaining_window.h`, which likely contains the necessary declarations and definitions related to the window and action chaining functionalities.
